_meta.description: Human-curated paper statements for Bridgeland (2007), 'Stability conditions on triangulated categories'. This file is the single source of truth for row-level data (prose, section, title, kind) keyed by paper reference. It is joined at dashboard-generation time with the `paperRef` field on `@[informal]` attributes in the project. The file knows nothing about Lean. Every numbered result in the paper appears here, whether or not it currently has a matching Lean declaration; results with no matching tag render as `missing` in the dashboard.
_meta._note_draft: SEED DRAFT — still requires human review before merging, but content is paper-sourced. Every `prose` field below was transcribed from math/0212237v3 (downloaded from arXiv and converted with pdftotext) and then lightly smoothed for running-prose presentation; Unicode math replaces the paper's LaTeX. No entry is speculative. Status does not appear in this file: per-declaration coverage status (`complete` | `partial`) lives on the `@[informal]` attribute as a separate field (added in a follow-up PR to lean-informal). Row-level status is derived at dashboard-generation time from the tagged decls' statuses.

kind.definition: A Definition environment in the paper.

sections.8: More on the space of stability conditions

statements.title: Stability condition on a triangulated category
statements.prose: A stability condition (Z, 𝒫) on a triangulated category 𝒟 consists of a group homomorphism Z : K(𝒟) → ℂ called the central charge, and full additive subcategories 𝒫(φ) ⊂ 𝒟 for each φ ∈ ℝ, satisfying the following axioms: (a) if E ∈ 𝒫(φ) then Z(E) = m(E) · exp(iπφ) for some m(E) ∈ ℝ_{>0}; (b) for all φ ∈ ℝ, 𝒫(φ + 1) = 𝒫(φ)[1]; (c) if φ₁ > φ₂ and Aⱼ ∈ 𝒫(φⱼ) then Hom_𝒟(A₁, A₂) = 0; (d) for each nonzero object E ∈ 𝒟 there is a finite sequence of real numbers φ₁ > φ₂ > ⋯ > φₙ and a collection of triangles 0 = E₀ → E₁ → ⋯ → Eₙ = E with Aⱼ ∈ 𝒫(φⱼ) the cone of Eⱼ₋₁ → Eⱼ for all j. The same definition is restated more compactly as Definition 5.1 in §5, after slicings (Definition 3.3) are introduced to package axioms (b)–(d).

statements.title: Central charge is a local homeomorphism on connected components
statements.prose: Let 𝒟 be a triangulated category. For each connected component Σ ⊂ Stab(𝒟) there is a linear subspace V(Σ) ⊂ Hom_ℤ(K(𝒟), ℂ) with a well-defined linear topology and a local homeomorphism Z : Σ → V(Σ) which maps a stability condition (Z, 𝒫) to its central charge Z. It follows immediately that each component Σ ⊂ Stab(𝒟) is a manifold, locally modelled on the topological vector space V(Σ).

statements.title: Numerical stability conditions form a complex manifold
statements.prose: Suppose 𝒟 is numerically finite. For each connected component Σ ⊂ Stab_N(𝒟) there is a subspace V(Σ) ⊂ Hom_ℤ(N(𝒟), ℂ) and a local homeomorphism Z : Σ → V(Σ) which maps a stability condition to its central charge. In particular Σ is a finite-dimensional complex manifold.

statements.title: Stability function
statements.prose: A stability function on an abelian category 𝒜 is a group homomorphism Z : K(𝒜) → ℂ such that for all 0 ≠ E ∈ 𝒜 the complex number Z(E) lies in the strict upper half-plane H = { r · exp(iπφ) : r > 0 and 0 < φ ≤ 1 } ⊂ ℂ. The phase of a nonzero object E is then φ(E) = (1/π) · arg Z(E) ∈ (0, 1].

statements.title: Semistable object
statements.prose: Let Z : K(𝒜) → ℂ be a stability function on an abelian category 𝒜. An object 0 ≠ E ∈ 𝒜 is said to be semistable (with respect to Z) if every subobject 0 ≠ A ⊂ E satisfies φ(A) ≤ φ(E). Equivalently, φ(E) ≤ φ(B) for every nonzero quotient E ↠ B.

statements.title: Harder–Narasimhan filtration and the HN property
statements.prose: Let Z : K(𝒜) → ℂ be a stability function on an abelian category 𝒜. A Harder–Narasimhan filtration of an object 0 ≠ E ∈ 𝒜 is a finite chain of subobjects 0 = E₀ ⊂ E₁ ⊂ ⋯ ⊂ Eₙ₋₁ ⊂ Eₙ = E whose factors Fⱼ = Eⱼ / Eⱼ₋₁ are semistable objects of 𝒜 with φ(F₁) > φ(F₂) > ⋯ > φ(Fₙ). The stability function Z is said to have the Harder–Narasimhan property if every nonzero object of 𝒜 has a Harder–Narasimhan filtration.

statements.title: Chain conditions imply the Harder–Narasimhan property
statements.prose: Suppose 𝒜 is an abelian category with a stability function Z : K(𝒜) → ℂ satisfying the chain conditions (a) there are no infinite sequences of subobjects ⋯ ⊂ Eⱼ₊₁ ⊂ Eⱼ ⊂ ⋯ ⊂ E₂ ⊂ E₁ in 𝒜 with φ(Eⱼ₊₁) > φ(Eⱼ) for all j, and (b) there are no infinite sequences of quotients E₁ ↠ E₂ ↠ ⋯ ↠ Eⱼ ↠ Eⱼ₊₁ ↠ ⋯ in 𝒜 with φ(Eⱼ) > φ(Eⱼ₊₁) for all j. Then 𝒜 has the Harder–Narasimhan property.

statements.title: t-structure
statements.prose: A t-structure on a triangulated category 𝒟 is a full subcategory ℱ ⊂ 𝒟, satisfying ℱ[1] ⊂ ℱ, such that if one defines ℱ^⊥ = { G ∈ 𝒟 : Hom_𝒟(F, G) = 0 for all F ∈ ℱ }, then for every object E ∈ 𝒟 there is a triangle F → E → G in 𝒟 with F ∈ ℱ and G ∈ ℱ^⊥. The heart of the t-structure is the full subcategory 𝒜 = ℱ ∩ ℱ^⊥[1] ⊂ 𝒟, which is abelian; the t-structure is bounded if 𝒟 = ⋃_{i,j ∈ ℤ} ℱ[i] ∩ ℱ^⊥[j].

statements.title: Characterization of hearts of bounded t-structures
statements.prose: Let 𝒜 ⊂ 𝒟 be a full additive subcategory of a triangulated category 𝒟. Then 𝒜 is the heart of a bounded t-structure ℱ ⊂ 𝒟 if and only if the following two conditions hold: (a) if k₁ > k₂ are integers and A, B are objects of 𝒜 then Hom_𝒟(A[k₁], B[k₂]) = 0; and (b) for every nonzero object E ∈ 𝒟 there is a finite sequence of integers k₁ > k₂ > ⋯ > kₙ and a collection of triangles 0 = E₀ → E₁ → ⋯ → Eₙ = E with Aⱼ ∈ 𝒜[kⱼ] for all j.

statements.title: Slicing of a triangulated category
statements.prose: A slicing 𝒫 of a triangulated category 𝒟 consists of full additive subcategories 𝒫(φ) ⊂ 𝒟 for each φ ∈ ℝ satisfying the following axioms: (a) for all φ ∈ ℝ, 𝒫(φ + 1) = 𝒫(φ)[1]; (b) if φ₁ > φ₂ and Aⱼ ∈ 𝒫(φⱼ) then Hom_𝒟(A₁, A₂) = 0; (c) for each nonzero object E ∈ 𝒟 there is a finite sequence of real numbers φ₁ > φ₂ > ⋯ > φₙ and a collection of triangles 0 = E₀ → E₁ → ⋯ → Eₙ = E with Aⱼ ∈ 𝒫(φⱼ) the cone of Eⱼ₋₁ → Eⱼ for all j.

statements.title: Phase bounds from distinguished triangles in an interval
statements.prose: Let 𝒫 be a slicing of a triangulated category 𝒟 and let I ⊂ ℝ be an interval of length at most one. Suppose A → E → B → A[1] is a distinguished triangle in 𝒟 all of whose vertices are nonzero objects of 𝒫(I). Then φ⁺(A) ≤ φ⁺(E) and φ⁻(E) ≤ φ⁻(B).

statements.title: Quasi-abelian category
statements.prose: A quasi-abelian category is an additive category 𝒜 with kernels and cokernels such that every pullback of a strict epimorphism is a strict epimorphism, and every pushout of a strict monomorphism is a strict monomorphism. (Recall that a morphism f is strict if the canonical map coim f → im f is an isomorphism.)

statements.title: Schneiders' characterization of quasi-abelian categories
statements.prose: An additive category 𝒜 is quasi-abelian if and only if there are abelian categories 𝒜^♯ and 𝒜^♭ and fully faithful embeddings 𝒜 ⊂ 𝒜^♯ and 𝒜 ⊂ 𝒜^♭ such that (a) if A → E is a monomorphism in 𝒜^♯ with E ∈ 𝒜 then A ∈ 𝒜, and (b) if E → B is an epimorphism in 𝒜^♭ with E ∈ 𝒜 then B ∈ 𝒜. When these conditions hold, the strict short exact sequences in 𝒜 are precisely the sequences 0 → A → B → C → 0 which are exact in both 𝒜^♯ and 𝒜^♭.

statements.title: Interval subcategories of a slicing are quasi-abelian
statements.prose: Let 𝒫 be a slicing of a triangulated category 𝒟. For any interval I ⊂ ℝ of length less than 1, the full subcategory 𝒫(I) ⊂ 𝒟 is quasi-abelian, and its strict short exact sequences are in one-to-one correspondence with distinguished triangles in 𝒟 all of whose vertices are objects of 𝒫(I).

statements.title: Skewed stability function
statements.prose: A skewed stability function on a quasi-abelian category 𝒜 is a group homomorphism Z : K(𝒜) → ℂ such that there is a strict half-plane H_α = { r · exp(iπφ) : r > 0 and α < φ ≤ α + 1 } ⊂ ℂ, defined by some α ∈ ℝ, with Z(E) ∈ H_α for all objects 0 ≠ E ∈ 𝒜. The phase of 0 ≠ E ∈ 𝒜 is then φ(E) = (1/π) · arg Z(E) ∈ (α, α + 1].

statements.title: Stability condition
statements.prose: A stability condition σ = (Z, 𝒫) on a triangulated category 𝒟 consists of a group homomorphism Z : K(𝒟) → ℂ and a slicing 𝒫 of 𝒟 such that if 0 ≠ E ∈ 𝒫(φ) then Z(E) = m(E) · exp(iπφ) for some m(E) ∈ ℝ_{>0}. The linear map Z is called the central charge; the nonzero objects of 𝒫(φ) are the σ-semistable objects of phase φ, and the simple objects of 𝒫(φ) are σ-stable. This is the compact restatement of Definition 1.1 using the slicing vocabulary of §3.

statements.title: Each phase subcategory is abelian
statements.prose: If σ = (Z, 𝒫) is a stability condition on a triangulated category 𝒟 then each subcategory 𝒫(φ) ⊂ 𝒟 is abelian.

statements.title: Stability conditions are the same as bounded t-structures with HN stability functions on their heart
statements.prose: To give a stability condition on a triangulated category 𝒟 is equivalent to giving a bounded t-structure on 𝒟 and a stability function on its heart with the Harder–Narasimhan property. The forward map sends σ = (Z, 𝒫) to the bounded t-structure 𝒫(> 0) whose heart is 𝒜 = 𝒫((0, 1]); Z restricts to a stability function on 𝒜 whose semistable objects are the nonzero objects of 𝒫(φ) for 0 < φ ≤ 1. The reverse map extends a bounded-heart stability function to a slicing on all of 𝒟 by shifting.

statements.title: Coherent sheaves on a smooth projective curve
statements.prose: Let 𝒜 be the category of coherent 𝒪_X-modules on a nonsingular projective curve X over an algebraically closed field k of characteristic zero, and set Z(E) = − deg(E) + i · rank(E). Applying Proposition 5.3 gives a stability condition on the bounded derived category D(𝒜). This example is studied in detail in §9 for the case of elliptic curves.

statements.title: Finite-dimensional modules over a finite-dimensional algebra
statements.prose: Let A be a finite-dimensional algebra over a field k and let 𝒜 be the abelian category of finite-dimensional left A-modules. Then 𝒜 is a finite-length category whose Grothendieck group K(𝒜) is the free abelian group on the (finite) set of simple A-modules. There is a group homomorphism r : K(𝒜) → ℤ sending an A-module to its dimension over k, and for any homomorphism λ : K(𝒜) → ℚ the formula Z(E) = λ(E) + i · r(E) defines a slope function on 𝒜. Proposition 5.3 then produces a stability condition on the bounded derived category D(𝒜) for each such slope function.

statements.title: A degenerate stability condition on a curve (motivation for local finiteness)
statements.prose: Let 𝒜 be the category of coherent sheaves on a nonsingular projective curve X as in Example 5.4, and let (Z, 𝒫) be the stability condition defined there. Let 0 < α < 1/2 be such that ζ = tan(πα) is irrational. Then the bounded t-structure 𝒫(> α) = 𝒫(≥ α) ⊂ 𝒟 has heart ℬ = 𝒫((α, α + 1)). Define a stability function on ℬ by W(E) = i · (rank(E) + ζ · deg(E)); all nonzero objects of ℬ are semistable of the same phase. Applying Proposition 5.3 gives a stability condition (W, 𝒬) on 𝒟 with 𝒬(1/2) = ℬ and 𝒬(ψ) = 0 unless ψ − 1/2 ∈ ℤ. This degenerate example motivates the local-finiteness condition of Definition 5.7.

statements.title: Locally-finite slicing
statements.prose: A slicing 𝒫 of a triangulated category 𝒟 is locally-finite if there exists a real number η > 0 such that for all t ∈ ℝ the quasi-abelian category 𝒫((t − η, t + η)) ⊂ 𝒟 is of finite length. A stability condition (Z, 𝒫) is locally-finite if the corresponding slicing 𝒫 is.

statements.title: Containment-radius formula for the metric on slicings
statements.prose: If 𝒫 and 𝒬 are slicings of a triangulated category 𝒟 then d(𝒫, 𝒬) = inf { ε ∈ ℝ_{≥0} : 𝒬(φ) ⊂ 𝒫([φ − ε, φ + ε]) for all φ ∈ ℝ }. Equivalently, the generalised metric defined by d(𝒫, 𝒬) = sup_{0 ≠ E ∈ 𝒟} max(|φ⁻_𝒫(E) − φ⁻_𝒬(E)|, |φ⁺_𝒫(E) − φ⁺_𝒬(E)|) agrees with the infimum of containment radii.

statements.title: Equivalence of stability seminorms on a basic neighbourhood
statements.prose: If τ = (W, 𝒬) ∈ B_ε(σ) then there are constants k₁, k₂ > 0 such that k₁ · ‖U‖_σ < ‖U‖_τ < k₂ · ‖U‖_σ for every U ∈ Hom_ℤ(K(𝒟), ℂ). In particular the subspace { U ∈ Hom_ℤ(K(𝒟), ℂ) : ‖U‖_σ < ∞ } is locally constant on Stab(𝒟), and hence constant on each connected component.

statements.title: Linear local model on each connected component
statements.prose: For each connected component Σ ⊂ Stab(𝒟) there is a linear subspace V(Σ) ⊂ Hom_ℤ(K(𝒟), ℂ) with a well-defined linear topology and a continuous map Z : Σ → V(Σ) which sends a stability condition (Z, 𝒫) to its central charge Z. The proof of Theorem 1.2 is completed in §7 by showing that this map is a local homeomorphism.

statements.title: Local injectivity of the central charge
statements.prose: Suppose σ = (Z, 𝒫) and τ = (Z, 𝒬) are stability conditions on 𝒟 with the same central charge Z and with d(𝒫, 𝒬) < 1. Then σ = τ.

statements.title: Deformation of stability conditions
statements.prose: Let σ = (Z, 𝒫) be a locally-finite stability condition on a triangulated category 𝒟. Then there is an ε₀ > 0 such that if 0 < ε < ε₀ and W : K(𝒟) → ℂ is a group homomorphism satisfying |W(E) − Z(E)| < sin(πε) · |Z(E)| for all E ∈ 𝒟 semistable in σ, then there is a locally-finite stability condition τ = (W, 𝒬) on 𝒟 with d(𝒫, 𝒬) < ε. (Providing ε₀ < 1/2 such a τ is unique, by Lemma 6.4.)

statements.title: Thin subcategory
statements.prose: A thin subcategory of 𝒟 is a full subcategory of the form 𝒫((a, b)) ⊂ 𝒟 where a and b are real numbers with 0 < b − a < 1 − 2ε. Any thin subcategory is quasi-abelian, and W defines a skewed stability function on it.

statements.title: Phase confinement for W-semistable objects in a thin subcategory
statements.prose: Suppose E is W-semistable in some thin subcategory 𝒜 ⊂ 𝒟, and set ψ = ψ(E). Then E ∈ 𝒫((ψ − ε, ψ + ε)). In particular the σ-phases of E are both confined to the interval (ψ − ε, ψ + ε).

statements.title: Object enveloped by a thin subcategory
statements.prose: Suppose 𝒜 = 𝒫((a, b)) is a thin subcategory of 𝒟. A nonzero object E ∈ 𝒜 is said to be enveloped by 𝒜 if a + ε ≤ ψ(E) ≤ b − ε.

statements.title: W-semistability is a property of the envelope, not of the ambient thin subcategory
statements.prose: Suppose an object E ∈ 𝒟 is enveloped by thin subcategories ℬ and 𝒞 of 𝒟. Then E is W-semistable in ℬ precisely if it is W-semistable in 𝒞.

statements.title: Hom vanishing between objects of different perturbed phases
statements.prose: If E ∈ 𝒬(ψ₁) and F ∈ 𝒬(ψ₂) with ψ₁ > ψ₂, then Hom_𝒟(E, F) = 0. (Here 𝒬(ψ) is the full additive subcategory of 𝒟 consisting of the zero objects together with those objects that are W-semistable of phase ψ in some thin enveloping subcategory.)

statements.title: Harder–Narasimhan filtrations for interior objects of a thin, finite-length subcategory
statements.prose: Let 𝒜 = 𝒫((a, b)) ⊂ 𝒟 be a thin subcategory of finite length. Then every nonzero object of 𝒫((a + 2ε, b − 4ε)) has a finite Harder–Narasimhan filtration whose factors are W-semistable objects of 𝒜 which are enveloped by 𝒜.

statements.title: Generalised metric on the space of stability conditions
statements.prose: Let 𝒟 be a triangulated category. The function d(σ₁, σ₂) = sup_{0 ≠ E ∈ 𝒟} max( |φ⁻_{σ₂}(E) − φ⁻_{σ₁}(E)|, |φ⁺_{σ₂}(E) − φ⁺_{σ₁}(E)|, |log (m_{σ₂}(E) / m_{σ₁}(E))| ) ∈ [0, ∞] defines a generalised metric on Stab(𝒟). The induced topology is the same as the one defined in §6.

statements.title: Group actions on the space of stability conditions
statements.prose: The generalised metric space Stab(𝒟) carries a right action of the group GL̃⁺(2, ℝ), the universal covering space of GL⁺(2, ℝ), and a left action by isometries of the group Aut(𝒟) of exact autoequivalences of 𝒟. These two actions commute. Explicitly, a pair (T, f) ∈ GL̃⁺(2, ℝ) acts on σ = (Z, 𝒫) by sending it to (T⁻¹ ∘ Z, 𝒫 ∘ f), and Φ ∈ Aut(𝒟) acts by (Z, 𝒫) ↦ (Z ∘ φ⁻¹, Φ ∘ 𝒫), where φ is the induced automorphism of K(𝒟).

statements.title: The space of stability conditions on an elliptic curve
statements.prose: Let X be a nonsingular projective curve of genus one over ℂ, and let D(X) denote the bounded derived category of coherent 𝒪_X-modules. The action of the group GL̃⁺(2, ℝ) on the space Stab(X) of locally-finite numerical stability conditions is free and transitive, so that Stab(X) ≅ GL̃⁺(2, ℝ).
